---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}
library(tree)
library(foreach)
library(doParallel)
library(pbapply)
library(ggplot2)

####### Simulation parameters  ###########
n <- 500
new_data <- seq(0, 1, length.out = 500)
seed <- 45

B <- 10000        # Paper: 10_000
n_sim <- 1000     # Paper: 1_000
noise_var <- .25
##########################################

theta <- function(new_data, df) {
  model <- tree(y ~ x1, data = df)
  pruned_model <- prune.tree(model, best = 5)
  predict(pruned_model, newdata = data.frame(x1 = new_data))
}

sample_indices_and_nbi <- function(n, B) {
  boot_indices <- matrix(sample(1:n, B * n, replace = TRUE), nrow = B)
  boot_counts <- t(apply(boot_indices, 1,
                         function(x) table(factor(x, levels = 1:n))))
  return(list(boot_indices = boot_indices, boot_counts = boot_counts))
}

bootstrap <- function(n, B, df, new_data, sample_indices_and_nbi, theta) {
  thetastar <- matrix(NA, nrow = B, ncol = length(new_data))
  res           <- sample_indices_and_nbi(n, B)
  boot_indices  <- res$boot_indices
  nbi           <- res$boot_counts
  for (b in 1:B) {
    sample_df <- df[boot_indices[b,], ]
    thetastar[b, ] <- theta(new_data, sample_df)
  }
  return(list(thetastar = thetastar, nbi = nbi))
}

inf_jk_bagged_variance <- function(n_bi, t_n_b) {
  B <- nrow(n_bi)
  n <- ncol(n_bi)
  t_n_star_mean <- colMeans(t_n_b)

  # Vectorized computation of the covariance matrix
  n_bi_centered <- sweep(n_bi, 2, 1, FUN = "-")
  t_n_b_centered <- sweep(t_n_b, 2, t_n_star_mean, FUN = "-")

  cov_i <- (t(n_bi_centered) %*% t_n_b_centered) / (B - 1)
  cov_vector <- colSums(cov_i^2)

  # Bias correction
  bias_correction <- (((n - 1) * B) / (B - 1)^2) * apply(t_n_b, 2, var)

  # Estimate of Jackknife-Infenitesimal Variance for bagged learners
  var_inf_jk_u <- cov_vector - bias_correction
  return(var_inf_jk_u)
}

# Function to run a single simulation
run_simulation <- function(sim) {
  set.seed(seed + sim)

  # Generate data with fixed x points
  x1 <-  seq(0, 1, length.out = 500)
  e <- rnorm(n, 0, sqrt(noise_var))
  y <- 0.7 * (x1 >= 0.35 & x1 < 0.45) + 0.7 * (x1 >= 0.55 & x1 < 0.65) +
    1.4 * (x1 >= 0.45 & x1 < 0.55) + e
  df <- data.frame(x1, y)

  # Bootstrap and bagging
  res1 <- bootstrap(n, B, df, new_data, sample_indices_and_nbi, theta)
  preds <- res1$thetastar
  nbi <- res1$nbi

  # Compute the Jackknife-Infenitesimal Variance for bagged learners
  var <- inf_jk_bagged_variance(nbi, preds)

  list(preds = colMeans(preds), ijk_var = var)
}

# Set up parallel processing
num_cores <- detectCores() - 1
cl <- makeCluster(num_cores)
registerDoParallel(cl)

# Export necessary libraries and functions to the cluster
clusterExport(cl, c("theta", "bootstrap", "n", "B", "new_data", "seed", 
                    "noise_var", "run_simulation", "sample_indices_and_nbi",
                    "inf_jk_bagged_variance"))
clusterEvalQ(cl, {
  library(tree)
})

# Start Simulation with progress bar
start_time <- Sys.time()
results <- pblapply(1:n_sim, function(sim) {
  run_simulation(sim)
}, cl = cl)
stopCluster(cl)
end_time <- Sys.time()

# Extract results
preds_list <- lapply(results, `[[`, "preds")
ijk_var_list <- lapply(results, `[[`, "ijk_var")
preds_df <- do.call(rbind, preds_list)
ijk_var_df <- do.call(rbind, ijk_var_list)

# Results for plotting
true_var <- apply(preds_df, 2, var)
ijk_var_mean <- colMeans(ijk_var_df)
ijk_var_std <- apply(ijk_var_df, 2, sd)

# DataFrame erstellen
data <- data.frame(
  true_var = true_var,
  ijk_var_mean = ijk_var_mean,
  ijk_var_std = ijk_var_std,
  new_data = new_data
)

# ggplot erstellen speichern
p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = new_data)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = true_var, color = "True Var")) +
  geom_line(aes(y = ijk_var_mean, color = "IJK Var Mean")) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ijk_var_mean - ijk_var_std,
                  ymax = ijk_var_mean + ijk_var_std,
                  fill = "+-1 Std of IJK"),alpha = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Legend", 
                    values = c("+-1 Std of IJK" = "blue")) +
  scale_color_manual(name = "Legend", 
                     values = c("True Var" = "black",
                                "IJK Var Mean" = "red")) +
  labs(y = "Variance", x = "x", 
       title = "Variance of Bagged-DecisionTree Predictions") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "white"),
        plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "white"),
        legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "white"))

ggsave("figure2_fix_x_tree_pack.png", plot = p,
       width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 600)

################################################################################
################################################################################
################################################################################

# Function to run a single simulation
run_simulation <- function(sim) {
  set.seed(seed + sim)

  # Generate data with new x points drawn from a uniform distribution
  x1 <-  runif(n, min = 0, max = 1)
  e <- rnorm(n, 0, sqrt(noise_var))
  y <- 0.7 * (x1 >= 0.35 & x1 < 0.45) + 0.7 * (x1 >= 0.55 & x1 < 0.65) +
    1.4 * (x1 >= 0.45 & x1 < 0.55) + e
  df <- data.frame(x1, y)

  # Bootstrap and bagging
  res1 <- bootstrap(n, B, df, new_data, sample_indices_and_nbi, theta)
  preds <- res1$thetastar
  nbi <- res1$nbi

  # Compute the Jackknife-Infenitesimal Variance for bagged learners
  var <- inf_jk_bagged_variance(nbi, preds)

  list(preds = colMeans(preds), ijk_var = var)
}

# Set up parallel processing
num_cores <- detectCores() - 1
cl <- makeCluster(num_cores)
registerDoParallel(cl)

# Export necessary libraries and functions to the cluster
clusterExport(cl, c("theta", "bootstrap", "n", "B", "new_data", "seed", 
                    "noise_var", "run_simulation", "sample_indices_and_nbi",
                    "inf_jk_bagged_variance"))
clusterEvalQ(cl, {
  library(tree)
})

# Start Simulation with progress bar
start_time <- Sys.time()
results <- pblapply(1:n_sim, function(sim) {
  run_simulation(sim)
}, cl = cl)
stopCluster(cl)
end_time <- Sys.time()

# Extract results
preds_list <- lapply(results, `[[`, "preds")
ijk_var_list <- lapply(results, `[[`, "ijk_var")
preds_df <- do.call(rbind, preds_list)
ijk_var_df <- do.call(rbind, ijk_var_list)

# Results for plotting
true_var <- apply(preds_df, 2, var)
ijk_var_mean <- colMeans(ijk_var_df)
ijk_var_std <- apply(ijk_var_df, 2, sd)

# DataFrame erstellen
data <- data.frame(
  true_var = true_var,
  ijk_var_mean = ijk_var_mean,
  ijk_var_std = ijk_var_std,
  new_data = new_data
)

# ggplot erstellen speichern
p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = new_data)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = true_var, color = "True Var")) + 
  geom_line(aes(y = ijk_var_mean, color = "IJK Var Mean")) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ijk_var_mean - ijk_var_std,
                  ymax = ijk_var_mean + ijk_var_std,
                  fill = "+-1 Std of IJK"), alpha = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Legend",
                    values = c("+-1 Std of IJK" = "blue")) +
  scale_color_manual(name = "Legend",
                     values = c("True Var" = "black", "IJK Var Mean" = "red")) +
  labs(y = "Variance", x = "x",
       title = "Variance of Bagged-DecisionTree Predictions") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "white"),
        plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "white"),
        legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "white"))

ggsave("figure2_new_x_tree_pack.png", plot = p,
       width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 600)
```
